paper_focus.title: High Temperature Oxidation of Iron-Manganese-Aluminum Based Alloys
paper_focus.research_objective: To determine the isothermal oxidation behavior and scale evolution mechanisms in Fe–Mn–Al alloys as a function of Mn and Al contents, and to identify the compositional domains yielding protective Al2O3 scales with or without chromium additions.
material_systems: Fe–Mn–Al–Cr quaternary alloys (e.g., Fe–10Mn–4Al–5Cr, Fe–15Mn–5Al–5Cr, Fe–7.5Mn–4Al–5Cr)
Ferritic (α), austenitic (γ), and duplex (α+γ) phase regions
main_process_variables: Oxidation time: 24 h, selected up to 100 h
Atmosphere: static pure oxygen at 200 torr
Specimen geometry: as-cast disc, ~7 mm diameter, 1 mm thickness
Quenching after oxidation for phase confirmation
